subject: add a python verification job in Integration repo (for vCPE)
commit_message:
add a python verification job in Integration repo (for vCPE)

Issue-ID: INT-1426

Change-Id: I90d6888dc23789da764b649704112548cbb2eaf1
Signed-off-by: mrichomme <morgan.richomme@orange.com>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

@bartek, I just configure the vcpe in a first step, it means that a tox.ini will be expected here by the verificaiton job

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

I'm not pretty sure this was the plan - my vision was to add this kind of testing facilities to repos first, at second stage fix all linter/pep8 etc warnings/errors and only then hook those tests in CI (so only when the code is refactored in scope of python verification jobs). If we merge it now then anyone including me will have to fix zilions off linter/pep8 related issues before being able to push any code at all. It concerns any other repos. That's why I opted for gradual deployment - verifiers in repos first to encourage commiters to gradually fix any verifier related issues and only then hook it into CI when the code is mature so that job doesn't block anyone.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

it was just a Friday test to evaluate the difficulty to include such verification in integration

we can see that for a 'pure' python verification we need to have a mvn-setting file, the LF jobs are not always obvious to follow...

I suggest that we do not merge this patch (at least for the moment) and start with patches in integration with local tox files

But as we can define subdirectories (to be verified once the mvn-settings will be added) we could add such limited verification based on the part of the code we can reasonably manage
Removed Code-Review-1 by Bartek Grzybowski <b.grzybowski@partner.samsung.com>

Patch Set 1:

I just added some tox/pytest setup in integration repo but without linters so i think we're good to go with merging this. Do you know how/where to add this mvn settings file or should i elaborate?

Also is it possible to configure this job so it runs tox for ALL tox.ini files it will find or do we need to set paths to each tox.ini explicitly here via "path" in submodule declaration?

I think we can have a look at https://git.onap.org/testsuite/python-testing-utils/
we introduced the tox and add the same issue
so we add the mevn stuff in the jjb https://git.onap.org/ci-management/tree/jjb/testsuite/testsuite-python-testing-utils.yaml
but it is fake
we could ask LF why they impose such file for repository not dealing with java...but it may take time
meanwhile I amend with a fake param
